verifier.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier (bare, soroban-sdk 26.1.0) — REDEPLOYED for risc0 5.0.0-rc.1 (GPU-unblock migration)
verifier.risc0_5_migration_note: risc0 3.0.5 -> 5.0.0-rc.1 to unblock GPU proving on Blackwell sm_120 (3.0.x sppark-0.1.15 crashed; 5.x risc0-sppark fork works). parameters.json change was MINIMAL: only control_root (a54dc85a -> 4ec91150); bn254_control_id + Groth16 VK byte-identical v3->v5. All 8 guest image_ids changed (new risc0 circuit) + all 7 contracts re-pinned via set_verifier+set_image_id (scripts/repin_v5.sh). v3 proofs do NOT verify on this verifier (different control_root) -> demo data + test fixtures must be re-proved with v5.

token.name: zkorage demo SEP-41 supply-tracking token
token.note: Provides the on-chain circulating-supply anchor (total_supply) the PoR policy binds to; classic SACs expose no total supply.

policy.name: zkorage Proof-of-Reserves policy (Week 3: on-chain history + upgradeable)
policy.image_repin_note: W5: re-pinned via set_image_id (tx 7180408a) after the guest soundness fix (assert pk==issuer_id); fresh PoR proof re-submitted (tx e60e9faa, history index #3).
policy.binds: journal.supply == token.total_supply() (cross-call); recomputes sha256(journal) on-chain; cross-calls the bare verifier
policy.history: append-only on-chain log: get_count / get_by_index / get_history(start,limit, MAX_PAGE=50); VerifiedResult.index; Verified event carries index
policy.upgradeable: admin-gated upgrade(new_wasm_hash, operator) -> update_current_contract_wasm (protocol-level WASM swap; storage preserved). New policy ID now; future changes are in-place.

gate.name: zkorage relying-party KYC gate (Week 5: identity selective disclosure)
gate.kyc_issuer_note: mock KYC provider (ed25519 seed [9u8;32]); distinct from the PoR custodian [7u8;32].
gate.binds: grants access to the public `accessor` committed in the proof (a Stellar account's 32-byte ed25519 key); subject_id is NEVER on-chain. Recomputes sha256(journal) on-chain; cross-calls the bare verifier. accessor fixed in-proof (stolen bundle not redirectable); accessor NOT authenticated (holder binds own handle); NULLIFIER DEFERRED.
gate.is_granted_note: is_granted re-checks the credential expiry against ledger time (live access decision); get_access returns the raw record regardless of expiry.
gate.history: append-only on-chain log: get_count / get_by_index / get_history(start,limit, MAX_PAGE=50); AccessRecord.index; AccessGranted event carries index. Access keyed by accessor: is_granted / get_access / get_latest_access.
gate.upgradeable: admin-gated upgrade(new_wasm_hash, operator) -> update_current_contract_wasm (same native mechanism as the PoR policy; storage preserved). Live upgrade proven: tx d2a7d1b23090e279c8a9dae210df22d89711c54cb2e2d1dbcb0a987dc91032a3 (is_granted expiry fix; get_count=1 + grant survived).

guest.name: claim_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, PoR/generic) — deterministic build; W5 soundness fix
guest.soundness_fix: W5: assert pk==issuer_id (the committed issuer_id must equal the key that verified the signature). Without it, a prover could verify under their own key while committing an allow-listed issuer_id -> forged claim. Changed the image_id -> policy re-pinned.
guest.build: cargo risczero build (Docker / reproducible); host embeds it via ZKORAGE_GUEST_ELF (methods/build.rs)
guest.deterministic_fix: pinned ruint=1.17.2 in methods/guest/Cargo.lock (1.18.0 needs rustc 1.90 > risc0 docker toolchain 1.88)
guest.rationale: policy pins expected_image_id -> all prover boxes must emit the same id; required for soundness (bare verifier is image-agnostic)

guest_identity.name: identity_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, KYC) — Week 5; deterministic build
guest_identity.predicate: verifies the KYC issuer's ed25519 signature; asserts pk==issuer_id, claim_type==3, kyc_status==PASSED; commits an 85-byte journal {result, claim_type, issuer_id, accessor, nonce, expiry} — subject_id ABSENT (selective disclosure).
guest_identity.build: cargo risczero build (Docker); host_identity embeds it via ZKORAGE_IDENTITY_ELF (methods/build.rs builds + embeds BOTH guests). ruint=1.17.2 pinned in guest-identity/Cargo.lock.

acceptance_testnet.case3_supply_mismatch: mint -> resubmit -> Error(Contract, #10) SupplyMismatch (verify Ok, binding rejects)
acceptance_testnet.cases_2_5_6: R<S (guest panic), issuer-not-allowed, expired -> covered by policy unit tests (10/10)

acceptance_testnet_week3.history_seeded: 3 entries / 2 distinct supplies: #0=1e13 (tx 478afeb4), #1=1.5e13 (fresh canonical proof, tx f220e4bd), #2=1e13 (tx 5021a139); ends at baseline 1e13
acceptance_testnet_week3.third_party_onchain_read: stellar contract invoke (public RPC, NO backend): get_count->3, get_history->3 entries
acceptance_testnet_week3.third_party_reverify: stellar contract invoke verify --seal/--image_id/--journal -> null (Ok); same checks the public /verify page runs in-browser against the public RPC
acceptance_testnet_week3.live_upgrade: no-op self-upgrade to same wasm (tx 39ef899b) -> get_count still 3, get_config intact (storage preserved)
acceptance_testnet_week3.submit_cost_note: first append (cold Latest/Result keys) min_resource_fee=636,680 stroops; subsequent appends ~237,973 stroops

compliance.name: zkorage compliance gate (Week 6: KYC ∧ not-sanctioned)
compliance.note: sha256 Indexed-Merkle-Tree non-membership in-guest; one proof = KYC'd ∧ not-sanctioned; subject hidden.

payroll.name: zkorage payroll gate (Week 7: confidential proof-of-income + auditor view-key)
payroll.note: in-guest ECIES (Option B): salary encrypted to the auditor's x25519 view key; public sees only ≥ threshold.

accredited.name: zkorage accredited-investor gate (Week 8: identity leg of the fundraising composition)
accredited.note: faithful clone of the W5 KYC gate (claim_type 7); proves 'accredited = yes' by an allow-listed provider; investor identity hidden; grant keyed by accessor.

fundraise.name: zkorage fundraise-access contract (Week 8: the composition finale)
fundraise.binds: request_investor_access(accessor) = is_revenue_verified() AND accredited_gate.is_granted(accessor) (cross-call, try_is_granted). can_access is the live read of the same AND.
fundraise.upgrade_tx: in-place upgrade (review fixes: try_is_granted + threshold-consistency); wasm_hash d0b298e625effb05cb6333f156104b1720e4c0eb355dce46294d88411cbb76a5; storage preserved.
fundraise.note: revenue REUSES the generic PoR guest (973c9831, value≥threshold); the financial proof is verified IN-contract and AND'd with the identity proof verified in the accredited gate. Revenue + identity both hidden; X public.

guest_accredited.name: accredited_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, Week 8) — deterministic Docker build
guest_accredited.predicate: verifies the accreditation provider's ed25519 signature over DOMAIN ‖ envelope (NEW-2 domain-sep, DOMAIN=zkorage-accredited-v1�); asserts pk==issuer_id, claim_type==7, accredited_status==1; commits the 85-byte identity-style journal (subject_id ABSENT).

dataroom_DR1.name: zkorage Confidential Data Room contract (DR1: data plane — upload/encrypt/store/anchor)
dataroom_DR1.binds: create_room(owner) then put_document(seal,image_id,journal,blob_pointer): image pin -> on-chain sha256(journal) -> bare-verifier cross-call -> result==1 ∧ claim_type==8 -> room exists ∧ room.owner.require_auth() -> dedup -> store Document {commitment via content_hash + ECIES disclosure eph_pub/ct/tag + recipient_pub + blob_pointer}. Upgradeable in place for DR2 (nullifier set) / DR3 (keyper grant) / DR4 (fact binding).
dataroom_DR1.note: DR1 is commitment-only (no attester); the seal guest ECIES-seals the doc key K to a recipient x25519 key in-guest (W7 Option B) and binds K to content_hash/room_id/doc_id (faithful). Blob ciphertext stored off-chain (Cloudflare R2, decided); content-addressed by content_hash. The on-chain proof confirms the disclosure is a real guest output. DR1 is plumbing; ZK becomes load-bearing in DR2 (anonymous eligibility + nullifier).
dataroom_DR1.acceptance_testnet: deployed + initialized (config: claim_type 8, seal_image_id 20adf66a, verifier CB75); created room 0101..01 (RoomCreated idx 0, tx 35f83058); put_document with a real worker/VM-proved seal -> DocumentAnchored idx 0 (tx 7e975410); on-chain verify of the seal bundle against the bare verifier -> Ok(null).
dataroom_DR1.backend_ch3: DR1 Chunk 3 backend (storage adapter R2+local stand-in, /dataroom/* routes, ECIES recipient opener). Full e2e on testnet via backend/scripts/dr1-e2e.mjs: R2 path (create-room tx c926eb94 -> seal proved_by=worker image 20adf66a -> put_document tx de0247d0, doc idx 0 -> recipient-open faithful+plaintext MATCH, wrong-key rejected); local-stand-in path (create-room tx cf8fe7e2 -> put_document tx 4ae04aa1). Storage: Cloudflare R2 primary when R2_* creds present (content-addressed by sha256(ciphertext)); local fs stand-in otherwise.
dataroom_DR1.sdk_mcp_ch4: SDK/MCP v0.9.0. zkorage-sdk: KEY-FREE recipient opener (recoverDocumentKey mirrors the seal guest byte-exact; aeadDecrypt = AES-256-GCM via WebCrypto, browser+Node) + reads getDataroomConfig/getRoom/getDocument/listDocuments + verifyDataroomBundle + decodeDataroomSealJournal. zkorage-mcp: 5 READ-ONLY tools (get_dataroom_info/room/document, list_dataroom_documents, verify_dataroom_bundle) — NO key custody / no open tool. Stable demo doc anchored for the read+open round-trip: room c1c33201… / doc 7cb3ee5c… (sealed to demo recipient 97a6b925…). SDK smoke + MCP selftest GREEN.
dataroom_DR1.frontend_ch5: frontend /dataroom page (violet black-glass): engine + upload/encrypt/anchor (worker-first) + the KEY-FREE recipient open running ENTIRELY IN THE BROWSER via the SDK (secret never transmitted) + public document browser. Playwright (real Chromium) GREEN; full suite 9/9.
dataroom_DR1.review: Ch3+Ch4 reviewed (security auditor + code reviewer) NO blockers + Codex CLEAN; review-hardening applied (commit 1c59645: @noble/curves pinned, gateway dataroom_seal hex validation [VM gateway restarted], self-consistent tag verify, length guards, fromHex hardening, prove-seal enqueue-before-upload). Ch6 review: contract audit SOUND for demo (no Critical/High; M-1 unbounded-growth + M-2 room_id-squatting are latent/DR2-scope, gated behind the trusted admin today) + frontend review clean (in-browser key-handling verified; secret never transmitted). Fixes: added the negative room-owner-auth test (contract now 16/16), Open-panel + upload hex guards, create_room doc note.

dataroom_DR2.name: DataRoom contract — DR2 anonymous-eligibility upgrade (membership + nullifier, in place)
dataroom_DR2.binds: request_access(seal,image_id,journal): image pin (MembershipImageId, fail-closed if unset) -> on-chain sha256(165-B journal) -> bare-verifier cross-call -> result==1 ∧ claim_type==9 -> room exists ∧ journal.eligible_root == room's EligibleRoot -> nullifier UNUSED in room (else #15 NullifierUsed) -> record nullifier + store Grant{accessor, recipient_pub, eligible_root, nullifier} + append grant log + emit AccessGranted. PERMISSIONLESS (no require_auth — the in-guest NEW-5 holder sig pk==accessor carries the accessor's consent off-chain). set_eligible_root(room,root) = room-owner auth; re-pin rotates the set + revokes stale grants via is_granted.
dataroom_DR2.new_storage: DataKeys added in place (DR1 Config untouched — no retype): MembershipImageId, EligibleRoot(room), Nullifier(room,nullifier), Grant(room,accessor), GrantCount(room), GrantLog(room,idx). New errors NullifierUsed=15, EligibleRootMismatch=16, EligibleRootNotSet=17.
dataroom_DR2.demo_eligible_set: zero-subtree membership tree (member at index 0, empties elsewhere); demo member id_secret 0x11*32 / id_trapdoor 0x22*32 -> id_commitment leaf -> eligible_root 8be678722c84e8bf478cd0c2a8e257bcc599f80d56ad2839e0188a1cace651da pinned on demo room c1c33201… (set_eligible_root tx d61eaf82). Demo room 0101..01 -> eligible_root dfee7aba (fresh id 0x55/0x66) for the cost measurement.
dataroom_DR2.acceptance_testnet: MARQUEE: two accessors from ONE credential (same nullifier 2d6ee934, demo room c1c33201). Proof A (holder 0x03 -> accessor ed4928c6, worker-proved) -> request_access GRANT (AccessGranted idx 0, tx 0b1b0357). Proof B (holder 0x04 -> accessor ca93ac17, SAME nullifier, VM-fallback-proved) -> request_access #15 NullifierUsed. is_granted(c1c33201, ed4928c6)=true, is_granted(ca93ac17)=false, is_nullifier_used(2d6ee934)=true, grant_count=1. The on-chain record reveals neither identity nor which eligible member.
dataroom_DR2.cost: request_access (room 0101, fresh proof C) via simulateTransaction: 35,046,471 CPU instructions (35% of the 100M budget; dominated by the Groth16 BN254 pairing), writeBytes 1040, minResourceFee 567585 stroops (~0.0568 XLM).
dataroom_DR2.contract_tests: cargo 31/31 (16 DR1 + 15 DR2: happy grant, nullifier reuse #15, different-nullifier grant, image mismatch, membership-not-enabled fail-closed, malformed/proof-invalid/result/claim-type, room-not-found, root-not-set #17, root-mismatch #16, root-rotation-revokes, set_eligible_root owner-auth, set_membership_image admin-auth).
dataroom_DR2.backend: DR2 Ch3: backend/src/membership.ts (sha256 tree + nullifier + NEW-5 sign + buildMembershipJob, byte-exact w/ guest) + eligible-store.ts + /dataroom/membership/* routes. e2e GREEN (scripts/dr2-e2e.mjs): fresh room -> 2-member anonymity set -> set-root -> prove worker-first -> request_access GRANT -> reuse #15 -> unregistered blocked.
dataroom_DR2.sdk_mcp: SDK/MCP v0.10.0. SDK: decodeMembershipJournal + isRoomGranted/isNullifierUsed/getGrant/getEligibleRoot/getMembershipImageId/getGrantCount + verifyMembershipBundle (no key custody). MCP: 5 read-only DR2 tools (get_membership_status/is_room_granted/is_nullifier_used/get_membership_grant/verify_membership_bundle), selftest 35 tools + no-key-custody asserted. Smoke + selftest GREEN.
dataroom_DR2.frontend: frontend /dataroom DR2 card (violet black-glass): engine + 'Request anonymous access' full ZK flow + read-only in-browser SDK status check. Playwright (real Chromium) GREEN; full suite 10/10.
dataroom_DR2.review: THREE passes, all clean. (1) security-blockchain-security-auditor SOUND (0 Critical/High; anonymity + nullifier + accessor-binding guarantees confirmed; byte-exact across guest/contract/backend/SDK verified) + engineering-code-reviewer Ship-it (0 blockers); fixes commit 0fb858e: gateway rejects leaf_index >= 2^20 (M-1, closes the reachable high-bit aliasing path; guest in-assert deferred — would change the pinned image_id), backend memberIndex capacity guard, corrected root-rotation comment (nullifier is root-independent), frontend label, stale gateway comments. (2) Second deep pass (two correctness agents): PROVER LAYER ZERO BUGS (byte-exactness re-confirmed across all 7 layers); off-chain fixes commit 3580e63: atomic eligible-store write (temp+renameSync) + fail-loud on corrupt file (was silent {} reset → would revoke grants), type-correct `as Uint8Array` casts, frontend 12-min poll unmount cleanup. (3) Codex review CLEAN. Deferred/documented: recipient_pub PoK is a DR3-boundary concern; permissionless front-run non-harmful (accessor sig-bound); demo backend mints/sees the witness (production = member-held, the only model under which anonymity holds vs the prover).

dataroom_DR3.name: DataRoom contract — DR3 threshold-ECIES committee upgrade (put_committee_document, in place)
dataroom_DR3.binds: put_committee_document(room_id, doc_id, content_hash, k_commitment, blob_pointer) -> RoomNotFound if room absent -> room.owner.require_auth() -> DocExists (#14) if doc_id already in the committee keyspace -> store CommitteeDocument{content_hash, k_commitment=sha256(K), blob_pointer} + append committee-doc log + emit CommitteeDocumentAnchored. NO ZK proof and NO on-chain key material here: K is Shamir-split off-chain to the keyper committee; faithfulness is off-chain (recipient checks sha256(reconstructed K)==k_commitment, then AES-GCM authenticates the blob vs content_hash); the ANONYMOUS access decision stays the DR2 grant (request_access / is_granted). NO contract change was strictly required for the keypers — they read is_granted + get_grant.recipient_pub; this method only anchors the committee doc honestly (vs a dead single-recipient seal).
dataroom_DR3.new_storage: DataKeys added in place (DR1/DR2 storage untouched — no retype): CommitteeDoc(room,doc), CommitteeDocCount(room), CommitteeDocLog(room,idx). Separate keyspace from DR1 Doc, so a committee doc and a single-recipient doc may share a doc_id. New reads: get_committee_document / get_committee_doc_count / get_committee_doc_by_index. No new errors (reuses RoomNotFound #12 / DocExists #14).
dataroom_DR3.acceptance_testnet: Upgrade preserved all DR1/DR2 storage (is_granted(c1c33201, ed4928c6) still true after the swap). put_committee_document on demo room c1c33201 (doc d3d3..d3, content_hash abab..ab, k_commitment cdcd..cd) -> CommitteeDocumentAnchored idx 0 (tx aed8e89b); get_committee_document returns it byte-for-byte.
dataroom_DR3.cost: put_committee_document via simulateTransaction: minResourceFee 375,360 stroops (~0.0375 XLM) — a pure storage write, no Groth16 verify (cheaper than request_access's 35M-instr verify).
dataroom_DR3.contract_tests: cargo 37/37 (31 DR1+DR2 + 6 DR3: committee-doc happy, room-not-found #12, duplicate #14, two-docs increment/enumerate, coexists-with-DR1-doc same doc_id, requires-room-owner-auth).
dataroom_DR3.key_release: OFF-CHAIN self-built 2-of-3 threshold-ECIES keyper committee (keyper/ package). Shamir GF(256) byte-wise t=2/n=3 (backend/src/shamir.ts); per-share ECIES reuses the DR1 keystream byte-exact, DR3 tag domain zkorage-dataroom-share-v1/tag (backend/src/committee.ts + keyper/src/share-ecies.ts). Each keyper independently reads is_granted + get_grant.recipient_pub from its OWN RPC and seals its share only to the proof-bound recipient_pub. TACo adapter NOT built (documented design only). Honest caveats: trusted dealer at K-split (DKG = hardening); keyper liveness (t-of-n margin).
dataroom_DR3.stable_demo: room 'zkorage-dr3-committee-demo' = a17388e8fe6c1ea798522f463d095e8267ca28d1c52b6a2a9977b2a42c40a8c8 / doc 'dr3-committee-welcome-doc' = 614664eba5e05904c98125425a8bd175f9908f74cab1fd4869143a0d1ebfe38a; demo member id 0x11/0x22 holder 0x03 -> accessor ed4928c6…; recipient = demo x25519 97a6b925… (secret = sha256('zkorage-demo-dataroom-recipient-key')). GRANT tx ac5ed077, committee-doc tx 40f59659 (content_hash 803355c8, k_commitment 98ba38b9). Reproducible key-free round-trip (SDK DEMO_DATAROOM_COMMITTEE). Seed: backend/scripts/dr3-anchor-demo.mjs (idempotent).
dataroom_DR3.e2e: backend/scripts/dr3-e2e.mjs GREEN on testnet: fresh room -> 2-member set -> DR2 proof (worker-first) -> GRANT (recipient = demo key) -> committee seal-doc (K split + dealt to 3 keypers + anchored) -> collect 3 sealed shares -> reconstruct 2-of-3 -> AES-GCM decrypt -> content MATCH; non-granted accessor -> 403 collect + 403 open; wrong recipient key -> not faithful. Keypers: 3 local processes :8801-8803.
dataroom_DR3.sdk_mcp: SDK/MCP v0.11.0. SDK (no key custody): committee.ts (shamirReconstruct, openShare, reconstructWithCommitment — byte-exact mirror, frozen-vector pinned) + openCommitteeDocument (collect sealed shares -> open with the recipient secret -> reconstruct -> AEAD decrypt; reads recipient_pub from the on-chain grant). MCP: 2 read-only tools (get_committee_document / get_committee_doc_count), NO collect/open/reconstruct/key tool (selftest asserts it).
dataroom_DR3.frontend: frontend /dataroom DR3 card (violet black-glass): committee status (3 keypers / threshold) + the demo doc's content_hash + sha256(K) commitment + the KEY-FREE in-browser open (collect via the backend aggregator -> reconstruct 2-of-3 -> AES-GCM decrypt; recipient secret never leaves the browser). Playwright (real Chromium) GREEN; full suite 11/11.
dataroom_DR3.reviews: TWO adversarial passes, both clean. security-blockchain-security-auditor SOUND (0 Critical/High; confirmed a non-granted party cannot obtain K, < t keypers cannot reconstruct, the share is sealed only to the proof-bound on-chain recipient_pub, Shamir GF(256) + information-theoretic t-of-n correct, no keystream reuse [fresh eph/seal], k_commitment hiding sound [K is 32 high-entropy bytes], put_committee_document honest, DR2 not weakened) + engineering-code-reviewer Ship-it (0 blockers; byte-exactness independently re-verified). Fixes (commit f786b01): keyper /deal FAIL-CLOSED when DEAL_TOKEN unset (M-02); SDK + backend opener read recipient_pub from the on-chain get_grant not the aggregator (L-01); seal-doc pre-deal get_room fast-fail + Promise.allSettled + WARN on partial deal (L-02); keyper selftest runs assertFrozenVector offline (drift gate); M-01 /share-oracle documented public-by-design (no identity leak; rate-limit = prod TODO); MCP version literal 0.10.0->0.11.0. Re-verified GREEN after fixes (backend self-test, keyper selftest, SDK smoke, MCP selftest, Playwright 11/11, targeted seal-doc room-check + open get_grant paths). THREE review passes clean: auditor SOUND + code-reviewer Ship-it + **Codex CLEAN** (third pass, 2026-06-16).

guest_dataroom_seal.name: dataroom_seal_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, DR1) — deterministic Docker build
guest_dataroom_seal.predicate: NO attester (commitment-only). In-guest ECIES (x25519, W7 Option B): seals the PRIVATE 32-byte document key K to recipient_pub; commits a 229-byte journal {result, claim_type=8, room_id, doc_id, recipient_pub, content_hash, eph_pub, ct, tag} (K ABSENT). tag = sha256(DOMAIN ‖ K ‖ content_hash ‖ room_id ‖ doc_id) binds the seal to THIS blob+document (faithful, non-portable). DOMAIN_KS=zkorage-dataroom-ecies-v1/ks, DOMAIN_TAG=zkorage-dataroom-seal-v1/tag.
guest_dataroom_seal.segments: 2 (measured; ECIES via curve25519 precompile, no ed25519). x25519 guest -> canonical image_id built ONCE on the VM and embedded everywhere (W8 cross-machine x25519 finding). ruint pinned 1.17.2.
guest_dataroom_seal.build: cargo risczero build (Docker); methods/build.rs embeds SIX guests via ZKORAGE_DATAROOM_SEAL_ELF (+ the other 5). host_dataroom_seal (job: doc_key_hex/recipient_pubkey_hex/content_hash_hex/room_id_hex/doc_id_hex; host makes the ECIES eph_secret). gateway kind dataroom_seal; worker 6th canonical bin.

guest_membership.name: membership_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, DR2 anonymous eligibility) — deterministic Docker build
guest_membership.predicate: NO attester. (1) NEW-5 accessor-auth: holder ed25519 sig over 'zkorage-dataroom-access-v1' ‖ room_id ‖ accessor ‖ recipient_pub, with pk == accessor asserted (off-chain require_auth, gasless/permissionless). (2) Membership: leaf id_commitment = sha256(0x00 ‖ id_secret ‖ id_trapdoor), fold a depth-20 sha256 Merkle path (internal = sha256(0x01 ‖ L ‖ R)) to the eligible_root. (3) Nullifier = sha256(0x02 ‖ id_secret ‖ room_id) (external_nullifier = room_id). Commits a 165-B journal {result, claim_type=9, room_id, eligible_root, nullifier, accessor, recipient_pub}; id_secret/id_trapdoor/leaf_index ABSENT (anonymous).
guest_membership.segments: 2 (measured; ed25519 holder sig via curve25519 precompile + depth-20 sha256 Merkle + nullifier). ed25519+sha256 only (NO x25519) -> canonical image_id REPRODUCES cross-machine (verified VM == WSL2, both 2f744f78). ruint pinned 1.17.2.
guest_membership.build: cargo risczero build (Docker); methods/build.rs embeds SEVEN guests via ZKORAGE_MEMBERSHIP_ELF (+ the other 6). host_membership (job: sig_hex/pk_hex/accessor_hex/recipient_pubkey_hex/id_secret_hex/id_trapdoor_hex/room_id_hex/siblings_hex/leaf_index; demo mode self-signs + builds a zero-subtree witness). gateway kind membership (+ public hex/u32 validation); worker 7th canonical bin. Worker-first proof verified on testnet (by=worker, bare verifier verify -> Ok).

all_contracts.note: Weeks 1–8 + DR1 — every deployed contract ID on Stellar testnet. Deployer GDLECNXD…GXZY6.

solvency_gate_BP3.name: zkorage solvency-gate (BP3 — a solvency proof that dies when you pull your collateral)
solvency_gate_BP3.auditor_note: mock bonded reserve auditor (ed25519 seed [19;32]); distinct from PoR [7]/KYC [9]/payroll [11]/accredited [13]/revenue [15]/appraiser [17].
solvency_gate_BP3.upgrade_note: third-review hardening: reject min_amount==0 on-chain (a zero floor is not a bond claim; closes a direct-submit bypass of the backend guard). Storage preserved (get_config + the demo grant on lock 4 survived). cargo 29/29.
solvency_gate_BP3.binds: submit_solvency_proof(seal,image_id,journal): image pin -> on-chain sha256(173-B journal) -> bare-verifier cross-call -> result==1 & claim_type==12 & auditor allow-listed & expiry>now -> journal.escrow==escrow_id -> journal.supply_token==supply_token_id & supply_token.total_supply()==journal.supply -> journal.bond_token==bond_token_id & escrow.get_lock(lock_id): active(!released & now<unlock_time) & revocable & token==bond_token & amount>=min_amount -> lock.depositor.require_auth() -> record keyed by depositor. is_granted(depositor) recomputes the bond liveness + supply + freshness LIVE (no stored grant expiry) => flips false the instant you unbond.
solvency_gate_BP3.acceptance_testnet: worker-first solvency proof (image d0a2f137, 173-B journal, supply 1e13, lock 2, min 5e8) -> submit_solvency_proof (tx 9df31c2a) -> is_granted(deployer)=true (SOLVENT). unbond lock 2 (tx 9737aa89) -> is_granted(deployer)=false (VOID), lock released. The self-void money-shot, on-chain.
solvency_gate_BP3.supersedes: the literal 'ZK proof tied to a classic Claimable Balance' idea (research-killed: Soroban can't read classic CBs + CBs are public = theater).

guest_solvency.name: solvency_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, BP3 solvency-bonded) — deterministic Docker build
guest_solvency.predicate: verifies the bonded reserve auditor's ed25519 signature over SOLVENCY_DOMAIN | envelope (DOMAIN='zkorage-solvency-v1\0'); asserts pk==issuer_id, claim_type==12, reserves(value)>=supply(threshold). Commits a 173-byte journal {result, claim_type, issuer_id, supply, nonce, expiry, escrow, lock_id, min_amount, bond_token, supply_token} — the first 61 bytes byte-identical to the PoR journal; reserves ABSENT (private). escrow/lock_id/min_amount/bond_token/supply_token are public binding values the GATE enforces on-chain.
guest_solvency.reproducible: ed25519 + sha256 only (no x25519) -> canonical image_id reproduces cross-machine; ruint pinned 1.17.2. Built on the WSL2 box (risc0 5.0.0-rc.1).
guest_solvency.prover_kind: solvency (gateway + worker, 9th canonical bin host_solvency); fields envelope_hex/signature_hex/issuer_pubkey_hex/threshold/escrow_hex/lock_id/min_amount/bond_token_hex/supply_token_hex; worker-first verified on testnet (proved_by=worker emitted the pinned image_id).

acceptance_testnet_week8.composition_positive: accredited grant (tx 17efe3b7 gate) + revenue proof submit (is_revenue_verified=true) -> can_access(demo)=true + request_investor_access GRANT (InvestorAdmitted, idx 0)
acceptance_testnet_week8.backend_e2e: fresh G-address investor (GABF456W): attest -> prove(worker-first) -> grant-accredited -> request-access -> can_access=true
acceptance_testnet_week8.worker_first_proof: accredited proof (image 04fe8d3e, claim_type 7, 260-B seal) proved by=worker -> verifier.verify Ok on testnet
acceptance_testnet_week8.upgrade: fundraise upgraded in-place (review fixes); post-upgrade can_access(demo)=true, is_revenue_verified=true, admissions=2 (storage preserved)
acceptance_testnet_week8.reviews: TWO review passes — security-blockchain-security-auditor SOUND (no Critical/High) + engineering-code-reviewer Ship-it; 7 fixes applied (try_is_granted totality, revenue X-consistency, MCP env, empty-input guard, fail-closed worker job-build, /prove-status PoR-cache guard, + the threshold u64-string). Codex review CLEAN. The 8-week build is complete and reviewed.

guest_docauth.name: docauth_predicate (RISC0 zkVM guest, DR4 document-authenticity / zkPDF) — deterministic Docker build
guest_docauth.predicate: Re-verifies a REAL third-party RSA-2048 PKCS#1 v1.5 (SHA-256, e=65537) signature IN-zkVM (m = s^65537 mod n via square-and-multiply on crypto-bigint U2048 risc0-fork; full EMSA-PKCS1-v1_5 structural check), over sha256(statement) which the guest computes itself; parses a fixed 88-byte signed bank statement (DOMAIN 'zkorage-bankstmt' | version=1 | field_tag=1 | account_ref(32,private) | value u64(private) | issued_at | expiry | currency) and asserts value >= threshold. Commits a 113-byte journal {result, claim_type=10, field_tag, threshold, issuer_key_hash=sha256(n), room_id, msg_digest=sha256(statement)}; statement/account/exact value ABSENT.
guest_docauth.cost_note: RSA-2048 modexp = 22 segments (~7x payroll); de-risked DR1 Ch0. NEVER crypto-bigint constant-time pow (329 seg).
guest_docauth.reproducible: RSA + sha256 only (no ed25519/x25519) -> canonical image_id reproduces cross-machine; ruint pinned 1.17.2.
guest_docauth.prover_kind: docauth (gateway + 8th worker canonical bin); fields n_hex/sig_hex/statement_hex/threshold/room_id_hex; worker-first verified on testnet (proved_by=worker emitted the pinned image_id).
guest_docauth.hardening_deferred: DR4 review LOW-1/LOW-2 (assert odd modulus + s<n) deferred to the next re-pin (non-exploitable under the trusted-admin allowlist model; would change the image_id).

dataroom_DR4.name: DataRoom contract — DR4 document-authenticity upgrade (attest_document_fact + issuer allowlist, in place)
dataroom_DR4.binds: attest_document_fact(seal, image_id, journal): image pin (fail-closed if unset) -> on-chain sha256(journal) -> bare Groth16 verify -> result==1 & claim_type==10 -> room exists & room.owner.require_auth() -> issuer_key_hash ALLOWLISTED (DocAuthIssuer; the third-party-truth trust anchor) -> dedup (DocFactExists #18) -> store DocumentFact(room_id, msg_digest) + log + event. Admin: set_docauth_image_id / set_docauth_issuer. Reads: get_document_fact / get_doc_fact_count / get_doc_fact_by_index / get_docauth_image_id / is_docauth_issuer_allowed. DR1/DR2/DR3 storage preserved (DataKeys appended, no retype).

acceptance_testnet.note: A REAL worker RSA proof attested an on-chain fact; non-allowlisted issuer rejected.

stable_demo.note: The reproducible demo fact (stable mock-bank key 3d181231; persisted in backend/data). The mock bank RSA-signs a private statement; the guest proves balance >= $X without revealing it; only an allowlisted issuer is accepted.

dataroom_DR4.reviews: security-blockchain-security-auditor SOUND + engineering-code-reviewer Ship-it (Ch6 fixes 0e946e2). LOW-1/LOW-2 documented as deferred hardening.
dataroom_DR4.sdk_mcp: v0.12.0 (read-only, no key custody: getDocumentFact/verifyDocauthBundle + 5 MCP tools)

dataroom_DR5.name: DataRoom contract — DR5 faithful disclosure / data-side teaser upgrade (attest_teaser + appraiser allowlist, in place)
dataroom_DR5.upgrade_tx_2_doc_hardening: ae9db2a9776543e2dc7643873e4bebad5d8d1e439679e21e66d5e33029f9e90e (Ch6 review fixes: doc-comment caveats on Teaser/is_teaser_valid + 2 tests; behavior-identical re-upgrade so source==chain; wasm b1f36915 -> 2fdd0597)
dataroom_DR5.no_new_guest: DR5 reuses the generic value>=threshold guest (973c9831, the teaser, claim_type 11) + the seal guest (8f24842d, the auditor faithful redacted view via put_document). No new guest / gateway kind / worker bin.
dataroom_DR5.binds: attest_teaser(seal, image_id, journal, room_id, doc_id): image pin == TeaserImageId (fail-closed if unset) -> on-chain sha256(journal) -> bare Groth16 verify -> result==1 & claim_type==11 -> room exists & room.owner.require_auth() -> referenced Doc(room_id,doc_id) exists (bind its content_hash = the released blob hash) -> attester (journal issuer_id) ALLOWLISTED (TeaserAttester; the appraiser third-party-truth anchor) -> expiry>now (Expired #9) -> dedup (TeaserExists #19) -> store Teaser + log + event. The attester signs nonce=field_tag (field semantics attester-vouched). Admin: set_teaser_image_id / set_teaser_attester. Reads: get_teaser / get_teaser_count / get_teaser_by_index / is_teaser_valid (expiry-aware) / is_teaser_attester_allowed / get_teaser_image_id. DR1-DR4 storage preserved (DataKeys appended, no retype; room_count=11 survived).
dataroom_DR5.cargo_tests: 69/69 (+18 DR5; +2 Ch6 review tests: same-proof-rebindable + field_tag-low-word)
dataroom_DR5.design: Auditor redacted view = a field-level disclosure policy (public/auditor/private tiers; PCI/HIPAA/GDPR masking; owner/custodian redacts) sealed to the auditor's x25519 key via put_document, integrity-faithful (content_hash + faithful tag). Teaser = a public ZK-verified fact (sealed doc's figure >= X, doc unseen) vouched by an allowlisted appraiser. Honest gap: redaction-correctness is owner-asserted (no new guest); the teaser figure is appraiser-vouched, the teaser<->doc linkage owner-asserted (owner controls the room) but bound to the anchored content_hash.

A REAL appraiser teaser proof attested an on-chain fact (figure>=$1M, figure PRIVATE); a SELF-MINTED appraiser key was REJECTED #8; an auditor opened a redacted view faithfully (PCI ****1881 / FOIA [REDACTED] / HIPAA year / GDPR drop), wrong key rejected.

dataroom_DR6.name: DataRoom contract — DR6 private-policy composition + revocation/rotation upgrade (the data-room finale, in place)
dataroom_DR6.upgrade_tx_2_review_fixes: fde41a615d9ee017c295980ad757f432bf34e3b2582f835f34da207a38e4cb71 (Ch6 review fixes: LOW-1 admission-log dedup + code-review #1 is_admitted revoke check for membership-optional rooms; wasm 1ac89c5b -> 7ca7aab8; storage preserved)
dataroom_DR6.upgrade_tx_3_review_pass_2: 5502d42da9f8728ec761368ff65e7990291b3d38d9bfb7cc81921a495b055e09 (2nd review pass: LOW-1 audit-log TTL fix [re-admit re-extends AdmissionLog/Count TTL, has()-guarded against legacy state] + EmptyPolicy #27 [reject a fully-open room: !require_membership AND no gates] + N1 doc + N3 backend gate-addr validation; wasm 7ca7aab8 -> 6571b74b; storage preserved, room_count=19 survived, demo still admitted, #27 verified live)
dataroom_DR6.wasm_size_note: Exceeds the legacy 64KiB guideline (the DR5 baseline was already 55KB; DR6's functions + structs make sub-64KiB impossible without gutting docs/functionality). Protocol-26 testnet accepts it — the 71,905-B raw upload (tx 6418152a) succeeded, so the actual max_contract_size is >= 71.9KB.
dataroom_DR6.no_new_guest: DR6 adds NO new RISC0 guest. The composite is an on-chain cross-call AND across already-proven legs (the W8 fundraise pattern, generalized): membership (DR2 guest 9550a12e) ∧ compliance.is_granted (gate CDSA3PUL) ∧ accredited.is_granted (gate CCLSXZBO). No new gateway kind / worker bin.
dataroom_DR6.composition: request_room_admission(room, accessor) — PERMISSIONLESS: load policy (RoomPolicyNotSet #21 if unset) -> not revoked (AccessRevoked #25) -> membership Self::is_granted (MembershipRequired #22, root- & revoke-aware) -> if policy.compliance_gate: GateClient.try_is_granted (NotCompliant #23, fail-closed) -> if policy.accredited_gate: try_is_granted (NotAccredited #24) -> record Admission + emit. is_admitted = the same AND, re-evaluated live (drops on revoke/expiry). The membership leg's NEW-5 holder sig (pk==accessor) carries consent off-chain, so admission needs no require_auth.
dataroom_DR6.revocation: revoke_access(room, accessor, revoked) [room-owner auth] toggles a per-room Revoked set; is_granted now returns false for a revoked accessor (so the DR3 keypers refuse shares + is_admitted drops) WITHOUT re-pinning the eligible root (others unaffected) or a guest change. The revoked member can't re-enter (room nullifier spent). is_access_revoked read.
dataroom_DR6.key_rotation: rotate_committee_document(room, doc, content_hash, k_commitment, blob_pointer) [room-owner auth] updates the CommitteeDoc in place + bumps KeyEpoch (CommitteeDocNotFound #26 if absent). Off-chain the dealer re-splits a fresh K' to the keypers + re-encrypts the blob; honest members re-collect new shares (grant still valid), the old K is useless. get_committee_key_epoch read.
dataroom_DR6.policy: set_room_policy(room, require_membership, compliance_gate: Option<Address>, accredited_gate: Option<Address>) [room-owner auth]. Per-room PUBLIC config (which legs apply); None gate = leg not required. The privacy is the requester's hidden attributes satisfying it. get_room_policy read.
dataroom_DR6.errors_added: RoomPolicyNotSet=21, MembershipRequired=22, NotCompliant=23, NotAccredited=24, AccessRevoked=25, CommitteeDocNotFound=26, EmptyPolicy=27
dataroom_DR6.datakeys_added: RoomPolicy(room), Revoked(room,accessor), Admission(room,accessor), AdmissionCount(room), AdmissionLog(room,idx), KeyEpoch(room,doc) — appended, no retype; DR1-DR5 storage preserved (config intact, room_count=15 survived).
dataroom_DR6.cargo_tests: 90/90 (+21 DR6: full composite, denial #22/#23/#24, revoke->denied+#25+unrevoke, broken-gate fail-closed #23, rotate epoch 0->1 + #26, owner-auth negatives, is_admitted totality, admission-log dedup idempotent, membership-optional revocation drops is_admitted + #25, + 2nd-pass: EmptyPolicy #27 rejects-open-room + allows-gate-only)
dataroom_DR6.reviews: security-blockchain-security-auditor SOUND for the demo (all 4 invariants hold: composite AND fails-closed on every missing/false/broken leg, revocation effective+surgical+re-entry-proof via spent nullifier, mutators room-owner-gated + admission safely permissionless, rotation owner-only/shape-preserving/denies-revoked; nothing cross-user exploitable; credential-pooling = documented by-design gap). engineering-code-reviewer Ship-it (byte/field + Option<Address> + composition AND + read-only MCP all consistent). Fixes applied: LOW-1 admission-log dedup (permissionless re-call no longer inflates the log/count); code-review #1 is_admitted explicit revoke check (write/read paths now agree for membership-optional rooms); #2 uniform accessorToHex across DR6 routes; #3 rotate-doc partial-redeal caveat; #4 frontend epoch label; #5 parseGateArg unconfigured-gate warn; SDK getAdmission/canAccessRoom doc hardening (LOW-2/INFO-1). Re-upgraded in place (upgrade_tx_2); full DR6 e2e re-run GREEN. **2nd review pass (deeper, both agents): auditor SOUND + code-reviewer Ship-it — confirmed the Ch6 fixes correct + reentrancy sound; found 1 NEW bug introduced by the LOW-1 fix (re-admit no longer re-extended AdmissionLog/Count TTL -> audit-log eviction asymmetry, audit-only) -> fixed (has()-guarded re-extend); + EmptyPolicy #27 guard (open-room footgun); + N1 stale-comment + N3 backend gate-addr 400-validation. Re-upgraded (upgrade_tx_3); cargo 90/90, smoke/selftest/Playwright GREEN, #27 verified on-chain.

acceptance_testnet.key_rotation: seal committee doc -> open MATCH -> rotate (epoch 0->1, tx 9c46bc2f) -> open with K' -> NEW content MATCH; revoke -> keyper share release 403

stable_demo.note: Reproducible idempotent demo (backend/scripts/dr6-anchor-demo.mjs): the fixed demo member (id 0x11/0x22, holder 0x03 -> accessor ed4928c6) ADMITTED under member ∧ compliance ∧ accredited + a committee doc for the rotation demo. Read live with canAccessRoom / isAdmitted.

dataroom_DR6.honest_gaps: (1) Credential pooling: each leg is bound to the accessor but the compliance/accredited gates only COMMIT it (no holder-sig there) — colluding parties could pool credentials onto one accessor; the membership leg IS holder-bound (NEW-5). Production fix = bind all legs to a shared in-circuit id_commitment. (2) Per-accessor revocation needs no guest change; full re-admit-after-rotation needs epoch-nullifiers (documented, not built). (3) The policy is public; only attributes are private (truly-hidden policies = a guest change, out of scope).
dataroom_DR6.sdk_mcp: v0.14.0 (read-only, no key custody). SDK: getRoomPolicy/isAdmitted/isAccessRevoked/getAdmission*/getCommitteeKeyEpoch + canAccessRoom (per-leg breakdown, reads gates at the policy-pinned addresses). DEMO_DATAROOM_POLICY. smoke GREEN. MCP: 4 read tools (get_room_policy/can_access_room/is_access_revoked/get_committee_key_epoch); NO admit/revoke/rotate/policy-set tool (writes backend-only); selftest GREEN.
dataroom_DR6.frontend: /dataroom DR6 card (composite-policy admission per-leg breakdown via canAccessRoom + live Numbers panel reading on-chain grants/admissions/key-epoch) + Playwright dataroom-dr6.spec.ts; full suite 14/14 GREEN.

dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.name: DataRoom contract — Pattern 2 prove-a-policy self-serve, PER-DOCUMENT access policy (in place)
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.wasm_size_note: stellar contract build --optimize = 73,051 B (DR6 baseline + the per-doc additions); testnet max_contract_size_bytes = 131072 (128 KB), queried live, so it fits comfortably.
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.no_new_guest: Pattern 2 adds NO new RISC0 guest. It composes the shipped DR2 membership proof + DR6 composite admission + DR3 threshold-ECIES key release. The contract change is additive: one write, two reads, one DataKey, one event.
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.datakeys_added: DocPolicy(room, doc) — reuses the RoomPolicy struct; additive, no retype; DR1-DR6 storage preserved (room_count=20 survived, demo accessor still is_admitted=true post-upgrade).
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.new_methods: set_doc_policy(room, doc, require_membership, compliance_gate, accredited_gate) [room-owner auth; committee doc must exist (CommitteeDocNotFound #26); reject empty (EmptyPolicy #27)] emits DocPolicySet. Reads: is_doc_admitted(room, doc, accessor) [effective policy = DocPolicy else RoomPolicy else bare membership is_granted; then the same leg AND as is_admitted] + get_doc_policy(room, doc).
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.keyper_gate: the DR3 keypers gate /share on is_doc_admitted (was is_granted); recipient_pub still from the DR2 grant. Backward-compatible (no-policy committee docs fall back to membership).
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.cargo_tests: 100/100 (+10 Pattern-2: full composite, doc-overrides-room, fallback-to-room, fallback-to-membership, revoke drops + restore, gate-only revoke drops, owner-auth, requires-committee-doc #26, empty #27, totality).
dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.acceptance_testnet: Upgrade tx 1b8a2cf0 preserved all DR1-DR6 storage (room_count=20, is_admitted(db16742c, ed4928c6)=true). Then set_doc_policy live (tx 172fcef0, DocPolicySet event: require_membership+has_compliance+has_accredited all true) on the demo committee doc; get_doc_policy returns {member, compliance CDSA3PUL, accredited CCLSXZBO}; is_doc_admitted(demo accessor ed4928c6)=true, is_doc_admitted(random 1111..)=false. The keyper gates /share on this.

stable_demo.note: Pattern 2 reuses the DR6 demo (no new proofs): the already-admitted demo member opens a committee doc whose per-document policy is member AND compliance AND accredited. Set with set_doc_policy; read live with get_doc_policy / is_doc_admitted; the key releases via the keypers (gated on is_doc_admitted) to the proof-bound recipient.

dataroom_pattern2_selfserve.status: SHIPPED 2026-06-19. Contract DEPLOYED + verified + demo doc-policy live; keyper (is_doc_admitted gate) + backend (doc-policy routes + wallet-owned rooms + /dataroom/rooms) + SDK/MCP v0.15.0 + frontend (Documents submenu, Browse = rooms-your-wallet-owns, dropped contents column, 'Open a shared document' reader flow folding DR6 Policy + DR3 Release) all built + self-tested: cargo 100/100, full Playwright 37/37, MCP selftest GREEN, SDK smoke GREEN. engineering-code-reviewer Ship-it (0 Crit/High; L2 hardening applied, L1/L3 documented). Pushed (branch + main, github.com/sillynerd45/zkorage, main db3e8f8) + FE+BE redeployed to the VM as frontend v0.7.0 (apizk Pattern-2 routes live, frontend fresh, .env/data preserved). NOTE: NO keepers run in production (confirmed via SSH), so the Pattern-2 key-release 'open' step is unavailable in prod (same pre-existing DR3 limitation); the policy/admission display works in prod. If keepers are deployed to the VM later, they must run the new is_doc_admitted gate.

escrow_BP1.name: zkorage Bonded-Proofs escrow (BP1) - Soroban-native time-locked escrow; gate-readable is_locked()/get_lock()
escrow_BP1.trust_model: no admin/upgrade; self-bond released by depositor (withdraw after unlock / unbond early if revocable); distinct-claimant lock is a non-revocable one-way send releasable only by claimant via claim; expiry enforced by stored unlock_time, not entry TTL
escrow_BP1.review_hardening: post-review (security-auditor + code-reviewer): H-01 withdraw/unbond self-bond-only + revocable-must-be-self-bond (closes withdraw-vs-claim theft race); M-03 deposit measures actual received balance (fee-on-transfer safe); M-02 TTL bumped on read so a watched lock is not archived; +9 tests incl. 4 negative-auth + H-01 rejections; 22/22 green
escrow_BP1.bond_token: fresh clawback-disabled instance of the demo SEP-41 token, symbol zkUSD, decoupled from the PoR supply token (CC3JKNC4...)
escrow_BP1.acceptance: on-chain smoke test PASSED on the hardened build: deposit 100 zkUSD revocable self-bond -> is_locked=true + escrow held funds -> unbond -> is_locked=false + escrow drained + funds returned (lock_id 1)
escrow_BP1.self_deposit_auth_note: deposit succeeds with plain source-account auth (--source covers both the escrow require_auth and the token.transfer sub-invoke); no signAuthEntry needed for self-deposit -> de-risks BP2
escrow_BP1.superseded_predeploy: first BP1 deploy (pre-review) was escrow CDZYQBRD... / token CC72G5DZ...; orphaned after the hardening redeploy
escrow_BP1.plan: development/Build-Plan/claimable/PLAN.md (BP1); next BP3/BP5 gates cross-call is_locked/get_lock
